description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with a single selenium stage. Define a hidden .selenium-template job that uses the $SCENIC_SELENIUM_IMAGE:$SCENIC_SELENIUM_TAG Docker image, runs on runners tagged with ${RUNNER_HOSTNAME}, and has a 30‑minute timeout; its before_script executes `cd $SELENIUM_DIR`, `pipenv install --dev`, `export DISPLAY=:0`, starts Xvfb with `Xvfb :0 -screen 0 1920x1080x24 -ac -listen tcp &`, sleeps 3 seconds, and writes a .env file containing SERVER_ADDRESS, SCENIC*_URL, SWITCHER*_HOST, SWITCHER*_PORT, USER*_SIP_SOURCE_PORT, USER*_NAME, USER*_PASSWORD, and PATH_TO_DOWNLOADS_FOLDER variables. The script runs a series of `$SELENIUM_SCRIPT` commands for each Selenium test file (e.g., `scenic_webdriver/scenic_boot_test.py false`, `scenic_webdriver/source_tests/user_should_be_able_to_instantiate_and_power_on_a_source_test.py false`, etc.). After_script sleeps 3 seconds and kills Xvfb with `pkill Xvfb`. It publishes artifacts from `$SELENIUM_DIR/log/*` and `$SELENIUM_DIR/recording/*` always, and uses services: scenic (`$SCENIC_IMAGE:$SCENIC_TAG`), scenic-core-1/2/3 (`$SCENIC_CORE_IMAGE:$SCENIC_CORE_TAG`). Then add a manual-selenium-from-branch job that extends the template, runs in the selenium stage, allows failure, has no needs, sets `SCENIC_CORE_TAG=develop` and `SCENIC_TAG=$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG`, and is triggered manually. Finally add a selenium job that also extends the template, runs in the selenium stage, needs the release-docker-image job, runs only on tags, develop, and master branches, and sets `SCENIC_CORE_TAG=$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME` and `SCENIC_TAG=$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME`.
